text.title: PM Certification Changes 2026 Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding PM Certification Changes 2026. Each pairs a binary check and a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Report MUST present ECO 2021 vs ECO 2026 weights side-by-side with delta column. Single-table-only reports forbidden.
rule.rationale: Single-table reports hide the change magnitude; side-by-side surfaces it for candidates and L&D teams.

rule.statement: Report MUST cite the effective date 2026-07-01 and clarify that exams before that date use ECO 2021.
rule.rationale: Candidates miscalculate exam scheduling otherwise; the effective date is the single most important fact.

rule.statement: Per-candidate-profile (early-career, mid-career, returning) the report MUST quantify added prep time required for the Business Environment shift.
rule.rationale: Unquantified deltas leave candidates planning blindly; quantification ties to study plan.

rule.statement: Report MUST list any new question types (e.g. drag-and-drop, hotspot, multi-correct) introduced in ECO 2026.
rule.rationale: Question-type changes affect test-taking strategy more than content; omission misleads candidates.

rule.statement: All facts in the report MUST cite the PMI ECO 2026 document or PMI exam release notes. Third-party blog posts forbidden as sole source.
rule.rationale: Third-party sources lag and contain errors; PMI canonical sources ensure accuracy.

rule.statement: If preconditions for PM Certification Changes 2026 fail (see Skip-If list in AGENTS.md), the operator MUST skip this methodology and document the fallback reason instead of fabricating an output.
rule.rationale: Applying PM Certification Changes 2026 outside its envelope produces false-precision artefacts. Explicit skip preserves epistemic discipline.
